reviewer.name: Anthony
reviewer.notes: Approved by Anthony for customer delivery after Rubric v1 evidence review, live-site scoped checks, schema/internal workbench red-team, and customer-preview red-team. Public sharing and logo authorization remain separate from this delivery approval.

indicators.label: Business identity
indicators.evidence: The title and visible page copy repeatedly identify Gleason Orthodontics.

indicators.label: Primary offer
indicators.evidence: The homepage title and body identify an orthodontic practice offering braces and clear aligners.

indicators.label: Intended audience
indicators.evidence: The About page states that the practice serves patients of all ages and families.

indicators.label: Geography and service area
indicators.evidence: Webster, New York and the South Avenue address are consistently present; the About page also references surrounding areas.

indicators.label: Contact path or CTA
indicators.evidence: The reviewed pages expose the phone, address, contact form, and free-consultation path.

indicators.label: Cross-page consistency
indicators.evidence: The same practice name, phone, address, Webster location, and orthodontic framing appear across all three reviewed pages.

indicators.label: Crawl access
indicators.evidence: The homepage, About, and Contact URLs returned HTTP 200 readable HTML without X-Robots-Tag blocks. robots.txt permits public crawling except wp-admin and declares a working sitemap that redirects to sitemap_index.xml.
indicators.collection_method: HTTP header, robots.txt, sitemap, and retained-page review

indicators.label: Indexability and metadata
indicators.evidence: The reviewed pages expose distinct titles, meta descriptions, index/follow directives, and canonical URLs.
indicators.collection_method: automated HTML metadata extraction + manual review

indicators.label: Identity and location consistency
indicators.evidence: The visible name, phone, South Avenue address, Webster location, and Orthodontist structured identity agree across the sample.

indicators.label: Appropriate local business schema
indicators.evidence: Orthodontist, PostalAddress, and GeoCoordinates markup exists, but identity nodes overlap, some records duplicate, and the practice node has an empty identifier.
indicators.reviewer_note: The 50% anchor applies because a usable business entity exists but duplication and ambiguous relationships remain.

indicators.label: Service-page coverage
indicators.evidence: Navigation exposes pages for aligners, braces, all ages, and airway/sleep-apnea services, but those service pages were not fetched in this targeted scan.
indicators.reviewer_note: The 25% anchor applies because service routes were discovered, but destination status and substantive content were not validated.

indicators.label: Internal linking and discovery
indicators.evidence: The reviewed pages contain clear internal paths to treatments, doctor information, reviews, financial information, first visits, and contact actions.

indicators.label: Entity and schema specificity
indicators.evidence: Orthodontist and doctor Person entities are present, but the practice and doctor are not cleanly separated and duplicate graphs add ambiguity.

indicators.label: Structured service meaning
indicators.evidence: No distinct Service or equivalent treatment-service entities were detected in the reviewed page markup.

indicators.label: Answer-ready content
indicators.evidence: The sampled pages give concise who, what, and where information and expose a three-step process, but no sampled FAQ or comprehensive question-answer coverage was retained.

indicators.label: Factual consistency
indicators.evidence: Practice name, doctor role, phone, Webster address, and orthodontic identity agree across visible content and captured structured data.

indicators.label: Authorship and provenance
indicators.evidence: Publisher and modified-date signals exist, but sampled content uses generic practice authorship and does not identify a clinician reviewer.

indicators.label: Machine accessibility
indicators.evidence: Readable HTML, headings, metadata, visible text, and JSON-LD were successfully extracted from all three sampled pages.
indicators.collection_method: automated fetch and parse + manual spot check

indicators.label: Verifiable contact and location
indicators.evidence: The phone number and complete Webster address are visible and consistent across the reviewed pages and structured data.

indicators.label: People and credentials
indicators.evidence: James F. Gleason and his orthodontic specialty and dental education are corroborated by ADA and Healthgrades records; direct NYSED license verification and every claimed membership remain incomplete.

indicators.label: Reviews and testimonials
indicators.evidence: The About page includes multiple named testimonial videos and a visible path to reviews and testimonials.

indicators.label: Outcomes and examples
indicators.evidence: The sampled pages contain general smile claims but no retained concrete case, before-and-after, or outcome example.

indicators.label: Affiliations and awards
indicators.evidence: A 2022 PepperPointe marketing award to Dr. Jim Gleason is independently documented; first-party professional membership claims were not independently verified.
indicators.collection_method: sampled external discovery + manual source review

indicators.label: Process and policy clarity
indicators.evidence: The homepage exposes a three-step path, free consultation, and links to financial, first-visit, privacy, and accessibility information, but those linked pages were not reviewed.

indicators.label: Third-party corroboration
indicators.evidence: A defined sampled external check found independent government, community, professional-directory, review, award, and nonprofit sources corroborating the practice. This does not measure backlink authority or completeness.
indicators.collection_method: sampled external discovery + manual source review
indicators.reviewer_note: No conventional press release was found. Backlink counts, authority, follow status, and link quality remain outside this sampled check.

indicators.label: Core services
indicators.evidence: The homepage and navigation enumerate braces, aligners, all-ages orthodontics, and airway/sleep-apnea services, but dedicated service-page depth was not reviewed.

indicators.label: Customer decision questions
indicators.evidence: The sampled pages address consultation, financing, treatment variety, and experience, but do not retain comprehensive FAQ or comparison answers.

indicators.label: Process and next steps
indicators.evidence: The homepage and Contact page expose how-it-works language, three steps, and a free consultation, but detailed process content was not sampled.

indicators.label: Cost, financing, or insurance expectations
indicators.evidence: The homepage references easy financing and affordability and links to financial information, but terms and insurance expectations were not reviewed.

indicators.label: Geography
indicators.evidence: Webster, the complete South Avenue address, and surrounding-area language are clear in the reviewed sample.

indicators.label: Audience and scenarios
indicators.evidence: The site identifies patients of all ages and families and names treatment scenarios including braces, aligners, and airway/sleep-apnea consultations.

indicators.label: Ownership, authorship, and recency
indicators.evidence: Publisher/author and recent modified-date signals exist, but authorship remains generic and no visible clinician reviewer is identified.
